description:
You are a data-analyst working from the command line.  
    Inside /home/user/data you will find three CSV files:

        /home/user/data/sales_q1.csv
        /home/user/data/sales_q2.csv
        /home/user/data/sales_q3.csv

    Each of those files is plain text and begins with the same one-line header “id,value”, followed by the data rows.

    Management wants a very small diagnostic snapshot that tells them, in one single number, how many data rows (not counting the header lines) exist in total across the three quarterly CSV files.

    Your task:

    •  Using **one** compound shell command (a one-liner; pipes and subshells are fine, but it must be entered as a single command line), determine the total number of data rows contained in all three CSV files combined.  
    •  Write that single integer to a new log file named
          /home/user/data/row_count_diagnostics.log
       in the following exact format (including the capital letters and the ‘=’ sign):

          TOTAL_ROWS=<integer>

       The file must contain exactly **one** newline-terminated line; nothing before it, nothing after it, no extra whitespace.  
    •  Do not touch any other files. No intermediate or temporary files should remain once your command has finished executing.

    Example (DON’T hard-code this value; compute it):  

        TOTAL_ROWS=23

    When the automated checker opens /home/user/data/row_count_diagnostics.log it will verify that:
        1. The file exists.
        2. It contains exactly one line.
        3. That line strictly matches the pattern “TOTAL_ROWS=<digits>”.
truth:
Initial files present before the task begins:

    1. /home/user/data/sales_q1.csv
       Contents (including terminating newline):
            id,value
            1,100
            2,200
            3,300

    2. /home/user/data/sales_q2.csv
       Contents:
            id,value
            1,400
            2,500

    3. /home/user/data/sales_q3.csv
       Contents:
            id,value
            1,600
            2,700
            3,800
            4,900

    Ground-truth totals:
        sales_q1.csv → 3 data rows
        sales_q2.csv → 2 data rows
        sales_q3.csv → 4 data rows
        Overall total → 9 data rows

    After correct completion, the file
        /home/user/data/row_count_diagnostics.log
    must exist and contain exactly the following single newline-terminated line:

        TOTAL_ROWS=9

    No other files in /home/user/data should be created or modified.
